summary: [deterministic] all deterministic checks passed for engineering-vllm-inference-blueprint | [judge] [fallback from codex] All nine success criteria are met. cargo check/clippy/fmt pass clean, 32 tests pass across config (6), health (6), billing (11), and server (8) modules. Config-driven vLLM endpoint via OnceLock, tokio BufReader stderr draining, semaphore concurrency limiting, and CI workflow are all correctly implemented. Prior round's three findings (silent u64 fallback, per-invocation reqwest::Client, opt-in billing) are resolved. Two low-severity design observations remain but are not code defects.

findings.title: max_spend_per_request and min_credit_balance config fields are defined but never enforced
findings.body: BillingConfig defines max_spend_per_request and min_credit_balance as configurable anti-abuse controls, but no code path reads these values. An operator configuring max_spend_per_request=1000 would get no enforcement. Either enforce them in the HTTP billing path or remove them from config to avoid false confidence. This is a design gap, not a correctness bug — the on-chain ShieldedCredits contract provides the authoritative spending limit.
findings.evidence: operator/src/config.rs:111-114 defines the fields. Grep shows they only appear in config struct definitions and test fixtures — never in server.rs or billing.rs logic.

findings.title: get_capabilities() is public dead code
findings.body: health::get_capabilities() and GpuCapabilities are exported but never called. detect_gpus() is used in main.rs and the /health/gpu endpoint, but get_capabilities() is scaffolding for future blueprint registration. Acceptable as public API surface, but technically dead code that clippy doesn't flag because it's pub.
findings.evidence: operator/src/health.rs:63-82 — only referenced in its own module definition, never imported or called elsewhere.
